name: Oklahoma Style Brisket Sandwiches
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the brisket in a Dutch oven.
2. Add the sliced onion, bay leaves, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and enough water to cover (about 6 cups).
3. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
4. Reduce the heat and simmer, covered, for 3 hours and 30 minutes or until tender.
5. Drain the cooking liquid and discard it.
6. Place the brisket and onion in a 2-quart rectangular baking dish.
7. For the sauce, stir together catsup, Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, coffee granules, and 2 tablespoons water.
8. Spoon the sauce over the brisket.
9. Bake, uncovered, at 350°F for 15 minutes.
10. Slice the brisket thinly across the grain.
11. Serve on rolls or buns.
12. Place any remaining sauce in a gravy boat for guests to serve themselves!
Makes 8 to 10 sandwiches.
Serve with "Salad Ole w/Chili Vinaigrette."
